title: Telemedicine | Aurora Derm
description: Teledermatology to orient the case, organize photos and history, and move into clinic in Quito when direct examination matters.
heading: Dermatology telemedicine in Quito

heroImage.alt: Telemedicine intake with clinical context and imaging

lead: Teledermatology works when useful photos, clear history, and one concrete question support diagnosis before deciding whether direct examination is needed.

thesis.title: Teledermatology to orient early and move into clinic when needed
thesis.body: Here we explain what helps to send, when remote care truly fits, when it no longer does, and how the case moves into clinic without getting stuck halfway.

statement.title: Teledermatology with clear limits, an in-clinic handoff, and a visible next action
statement.alt: Remote dermatology review with follow-up and adjustment

internalMessage.description: Remote guidance works when there is a good history, useful photos, and a concrete question; if warning signs or meaningful uncertainty appear, we move quickly into clinic.

blocks.title: What helps to send
items: Photos with good light, one context image, and one closer image of the area in question.
How long it has been happening, which symptoms changed, and what treatment you already tried.
Relevant history such as allergies, pregnancy, breastfeeding, or current medication.
One concrete question: whether it makes sense to wait, adjust, schedule clinic, or change routes.

blocks.title: When it does fit
items: Known flares that need adjustment without waiting for a full clinic visit.
Questions between reviews when progression can be compared through photos and history.
Follow-up after a recent treatment or routine change.
Early orientation when you need to know whether arranging clinic soon makes sense.

blocks.title: When to move into clinic
items: Blurred images or symptoms that still lack enough context.
New lesions with rapid change, bleeding, drainage, intense pain, or functional impact.
Likely need for dermatoscopy, a procedure, or direct physical examination.
When the first remote read leaves important clinical doubt or the first plan does not respond as expected.

initiatives.title: Take clear photos
initiatives.copy: Natural light, steady distance, and one context photo make the first read much clearer from the start.

initiatives.title: Explain what changed
initiatives.copy: Timing, symptoms, and previous treatment help decide whether this remote start is enough or whether clinic is already the better move.

initiatives.title: Watch clinic signals
initiatives.copy: Pain, bleeding, fast spread, or poor response are signs to move from remote care into clinic without stretching the wait.

initiatives.title: Message us on WhatsApp
initiatives.copy: If you already need help choosing the next action, we can tell you on WhatsApp whether to stay remote or move into clinic.

bookingStatus.title: Online booking under maintenance
bookingStatus.description: If this guidance shows direct examination matters, message us on WhatsApp to place the right clinic visit in Quito. If remote care still fits, we ask for clearer photos.
